name: Accessibility Request

assignees: 'ccere'

---

## ♿ Accessibility Request

### What accessibility issue are you experiencing?

**Type of Issue:**
- [ ] Visual accessibility (color contrast, font size, layout)
- [ ] Keyboard navigation (tab order, focus indicators)
- [ ] Screen reader support (ARIA labels, semantic HTML)
- [ ] Audio accessibility (captions, audio descriptions)
- [ ] Motor accessibility (click targets, timing)
- [ ] Cognitive accessibility (clear language, simple navigation)
- [ ] Other (describe)

**Severity:**
- [ ] Critical (completely unusable)
- [ ] High (major difficulty using)
- [ ] Medium (some difficulty using)
- [ ] Low (minor inconvenience)

### What are you trying to do?

<!-- Describe the task or action you're trying to accomplish -->

### What is the expected behavior?

<!-- Describe what you expected to happen -->

### What is the actual behavior?

<!-- Describe what actually happened -->

### Your Accessibility Needs

**Assistive Technology Used:**
- [ ] Screen reader (specify: NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver, etc.)
- [ ] Screen magnifier
- [ ] High contrast mode
- [ ] Keyboard-only navigation
- [ ] Voice control software
- [ ] Switch control
- [ ] Other (describe)

**Specific Requirements:**
- [ ] High contrast colors
- [ ] Large text options
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts
- [ ] Audio feedback
- [ ] Haptic feedback
- [ ] Simplified interface
- [ ] Other (describe)

**Who is affected?**
- [ ] Users with visual impairments
- [ ] Users with motor impairments
- [ ] Users with hearing impairments

- [ ] Users with temporary disabilities
- [ ] All users (universal design)

**How many users are affected?**

- [ ] Small group
- [ ] Large group
- [ ] Unknown
- [ ] Potentially many

## 📋 Checklist

Before submitting, please ensure you have:

- [ ] Described the accessibility issue clearly
- [ ] Specified your assistive technology needs
- [ ] Provided examples of problematic elements
- [ ] Described the impact on users
- [ ] Suggested potential solutions
- [ ] Indicated your ability to help test

## 🔧 Accessibility Standards

**DXPR follows these accessibility guidelines:**

1. **WCAG 2.1 AA**: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines
2. **Section 508**: US federal accessibility requirements
3. **Universal Design**: Accessible to all users
4. **Keyboard Navigation**: Full functionality without mouse
5. **Screen Reader Support**: Proper ARIA labels and semantic HTML

**Current Accessibility Features:**

- [ ] Keyboard navigation support
- [ ] High contrast mode
- [ ] Screen reader compatibility
- [ ] Focus indicators
- [ ] Semantic HTML structure
- [ ] ARIA labels and roles

## 📚 Resources

- **WCAG Guidelines**: [https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/quickref/](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/quickref/)
- **ARIA Best Practices**: [https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/)
- **Accessibility Testing**: [https://www.w3.org/WAI/ER/tools/](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ER/tools/)
